Question: Should EAD_MATRIX_ID include RATE_TYPE?
Engine behaviour today: IDs include rate type, e.g. BCEF_CONSO_TF_Q.
What we need from the business: Confirm the spec form (with RATE_TYPE) is the production-canonical ID.
Decision so far / answer: DECISION (user): keep spec form with RATE_TYPE; real BNP output uses it too.

Question: Aggregated segment name (INVEST_PRO + INVEST_CORP -> INVEST)
Engine behaviour today: Uses AGGREGATED_SEGMENT_NAME when AGGREGATION=YES.
What we need from the business: Confirm the aggregation rule stays.

Question: FWL flag for an aggregated matrix when constituents disagree
Engine behaviour today: FWL=YES if ANY constituent is YES (so INVEST=YES).
What we need from the business: Confirm the any-YES rule.

Question: Emit both Q and Y for every matrix?
Engine behaviour today: Always emits both Q and Y.
What we need from the business: Confirm both are always emitted (vs one selected per matrix).

Question: Source of FWL_TO_BE_APPLIED; is CONSO forward-looking?
Engine behaviour today: Read from PARAMETRAGE; CONSO set to YES.
What we need from the business: Confirm CONSO FWL=YES (already business-confirmed).

Question: Core RA formula
Engine behaviour today: RA = -(RA_STAT + RA_FI + RE) / CRD (baseline).
What we need from the business: Confirm the formula.

Question: Period aggregation windows (quarterly / yearly)
Engine behaviour today: Quarterly: RA metrics half-weight window, CRD block mean of 3 months. Yearly: sums; CRD mean.
What we need from the business: Confirm windows. NOTE: CRD and RA-metric windows are offset, which causes the tail spike in Q30.

Question: Handling when CRD = 0
Engine behaviour today: CRD=0 -> RA=0 (curve flattens) instead of dividing by zero.
What we need from the business: Confirm. NOTE: this only covers EXACT zero; non-zero residual / cliff is Q26 and Q30.

Question: Flat-tail boundary
Engine behaviour today: Compute to the last computable term, then hold flat to 50 and 100.
What we need from the business: Confirm the flat-tail rule.

Question: Stress leg selection (+ / -) under FWL=YES
Engine behaviour today: delta<0 -> STRESS(-); delta>=0 -> STRESS(+), by sign of the macro rate move.
What we need from the business: Confirm leg-by-sign rule.

Question: Macro shock: single date or path over a window?
Engine behaviour today: Macro path over a shock window; term 0 = window start, step 1 quarter.
What we need from the business: Confirm the window anchor for the forward-looking file (see Q25).

Question: ref_shock calibration (shock magnitude)
Engine behaviour today: ref_shock = 1.0, i.e. the shock is effectively off.
What we need from the business: Provide a calibration value or a trusted reference output to set the magnitude.

Question: Macro-delta x100 scaling?
Engine behaviour today: Delta kept raw; any x100 / unit scaling is absorbed by ref_shock.
What we need from the business: Confirm the raw-delta convention.

Question: Scenario S (Secto): out of scope? (merges former Q24)
Engine behaviour today: Not implemented (no Secto data in the scenario file, no S rows in target).
What we need from the business: Confirm Secto is out of scope for this delivery. One-line addition ('Secto' -> 'S') once data exists.

Question: Adverse vs Extreme differentiation
Engine behaviour today: New scenario file gives distinct A/E deltas; engine emits distinct A/O/E.
What we need from the business: Confirm the new scenario values are the intended ones.
Decision so far / answer: RESOLVED via Scenario_EAD_FWD.xlsx (A ~ -0.0048, E ~ -0.0068 on IR_10Y_FR).

Question: Number format (delimiter, decimal comma, decimals)
Engine behaviour today: Semicolon delimiter, decimal comma, EAD_RA_RATE 9 dp half-up, trailing zeros stripped.
What we need from the business: Confirm the output format.

Question: Spec says 'no difference between scenarios' under FWL=YES
Engine behaviour today: FWL=YES scenarios DO differ (scenario-shocked).
What we need from the business: Confirm (already business-confirmed); spec sentence applies only to FWL=NO.

Question: Role of PROJECTION_HORIZON (= 3Y in PARAMETRAGE)
Engine behaviour today: Column is present but NOT used by the engine.
What we need from the business: Does PROJECTION_HORIZON cap the curve (then hold flat), or is it informational?

Question: Canonical production ID form
Engine behaviour today: IDs include RATE_TYPE.
What we need from the business: Confirm production-canonical ID = with RATE_TYPE.
Decision so far / answer: EVIDENCE: real BNP output uses BCEF_*_TF_*. (Local validation harness id-map needs fixing - Q28.)

Question: Multiple rate types: separate or combined matrices?
Engine behaviour today: Each rate type is treated as a separate matrix.
What we need from the business: When TV (and others) exist, separate matrix per rate type or combined per segment?

Question: Shock scope for aggregated segments (INVEST = PRO + CORP)
Engine behaviour today: The whole combined PRO+CORP series is shocked.
What we need from the business: Should only the FWL=YES constituent (CORP) be shocked? Affects shock magnitude.

Question: computation_frequency: Q, Y, or both?
Engine behaviour today: Always emits both Q and Y.
What we need from the business: Confirm the production rule (always both, or per-matrix selection).

Question: The term 100 point
Engine behaviour today: After 50 there is a single term 100 holding the flat value.
What we need from the business: Is term 100 a genuine reporting point (perpetuity proxy) or a sentinel?

Question: Per-scenario Excel workbook + forward-looking window
Engine behaviour today: Reads one sheet per scenario (Central/Adverse/Optimistic/Extreme); window 2025Q4..2028Q4.
What we need from the business: Confirm: (1) one-sheet-per-scenario is the production layout; (2) what is term 0 for a forward-looking scenario; (3) extra macro columns (CPI/CRE) are ignored.

Question: Constant vs decaying RA_FI/RE; run-off at a non-zero CRD residual
Engine behaviour today: With constant RA_FI/RE in v2, RA balloons as CRD runs off; MORTGAGE/INVEST over-decay in the tail.
What we need from the business: In real data, are MORTGAGE/INVEST RA_FI and RE CONSTANT across all months, or do they DECAY with the balance? Should run-off freeze at a non-zero CRD residual?
Decision so far / answer: Pending business decision (drives the whole MORTGAGE divergence).

Question: Label / locale robustness (NBSP, underscore, label typo)
Engine behaviour today: canon() normalizes FWL_TYPE/METRIC/SEGMENT/RATE_TYPE labels; a diagnostic warns on a missing stress leg.
What we need from the business: Confirm the canonical label vocabulary (BASELINE; STRESS (+); STRESS (-); CRD; RA STAT; RA FI; RE).
Decision so far / answer: Engine hardened; normalization cannot catch a semantically WRONG label (e.g. BASELINE (+) typo).

Question: Cross-environment naming + fix the validation harness
Engine behaviour today: Names differ by environment: TF vs ITF; IR_10Y_FR vs IR_10Y_FRI; INVEST_PRO vs INVEST_PROD.
What we need from the business: Are per-environment names expected, or is a canonical mapping needed? Also fix the local harness id-map.

Question: Perimeter scope (BCEF only, or BGL/BNL/FORTIS/BPOST/BPLS too?)
Engine behaviour today: Engine reads only RA_BCEF; other perimeters are disabled. PARAMETRAGE defines BGL/BNL (macro BE/IT), FORTIS/BPOST, and ~50 BPLS rows (numeric segments, blank RATE_TYPE).
What we need from the business: Which perimeters are in scope? Confirm per-perimeter macro mapping (FR/BE/IT). How to name BPLS rows with a numeric segment and no rate type?

Question: Run-off freeze rule on the deep-tail cliff (guard added 2026-06-04)
Engine behaviour today: RA>=1 (cliff) -> freeze EAD at the last good value; output clamped to [0,1]. Stops the negative blow-up (e.g. INVEST -389).
What we need from the business: Confirm freeze-at-last-value is intended (vs cap RA<1, RA=0-and-continue, or a fixed projection horizon). Should the offset CRD vs RA windows (Q7) be aligned?
Decision so far / answer: Guard implemented to bound output; intended behaviour to be confirmed.

Question: PARAMETRAGE source of truth
Engine behaviour today: Config uses PARAMETRAGE_corrected.xlsx (hand-patched: added MORTGAGE row, full-precision values).
What we need from the business: Confirm PARAMETRAGE_corrected is authoritative, or specify how the production PARAMETRAGE is delivered.

Question: FWL=NO STEP 3: DET RA FI / DET RE computed but not used
Engine behaviour today: Engine sums all three details: RA = -(RA_STAT + RA_FI + RE)/CRD (matches target).
What we need from the business: Confirm ticket STEP 3 should be VECTOR = 1 - (DET RA STAT + DET RA FI + DET RE), not RA STAT alone.
Decision so far / answer: Ticket-text defect: STEP 2 computes DET RA FI/RE but STEP 3 uses only DET RA STAT. Engine already sums all three.

Question: FWL=YES STEP 4/5: scenario FI+RE detail undefined; (CENTRAL) base + missing sign
Engine behaviour today: scenarioRa: scenario FI+RE = fireBase + weight*(fireStress - fireBase); fireBase = -(FI_base+RE_base)/CRD_base (= Central); leg by sign of macro delta.
What we need from the business: Confirm: (a) the explicit shocked FI+RE formula for A/O/E (STEP 5 uses RA FI RE (SCENARIO), never defined); (b) base labelled CENTRAL not BASELINE (same value); (c) RA FI RE (CENTRAL) needs the leading minus.
Decision so far / answer: Ticket-text defects; engine implements the scenario shock correctly.
